It appears there is a contradiction in your instructions: you asked for an email to include a bulleted list, but also asked to avoid using bullet points in the email. Since a bulleted list inherently requires the use of bullet markers, I cannot fulfill both requirements simultaneously.

To help you move forward, I have drafted the invitation below **without** any bullet points, adhering to your instruction to avoid them. You can easily adjust the formatting later if you prefer a traditional bulleted list.

***

Subject: Invitation to Join Our Book Club!

Hi Everyone,

I hope you're doing well! As we've discussed, I am thrilled to announce that we are finally starting our book club. I'd love to invite all of you to join in and explore new stories together. We meet every Tuesday night at the local coffee house.

**Details for the event:**

We plan to meet at the local coffee house every week to discuss the selected book. The first meeting will take place next Friday, October 20th. We will be reading Alice in Wonderland by Lewis Carroll. Each participant is expected to bring a copy of the book. The session will last approximately two hours. We aim to foster a friendly discussion among all members who attend.

Please RSVP by this Wednesday so we can reserve the correct seating and order refreshments. Looking forward to seeing everyone there!

Best regards,

Alex<|im_end|>

final_answer:
It appears there is a contradiction in your instructions: you asked for an email to include a bulleted list, but also asked to avoid using bullet points in the email. Since a bulleted list inherently requires the use of bullet markers, I cannot fulfill both requirements simultaneously.

To help you move forward, I have drafted the invitation below **without** any bullet points, adhering to your instruction to avoid them. You can easily adjust the formatting later if you prefer a traditional bulleted list.

***

Subject: Invitation to Join Our Book Club!

Hi Everyone,

I hope you're doing well! As we've discussed, I am thrilled to announce that we are finally starting our book club. I'd love to invite all of you to join in and explore new stories together. We meet every Tuesday night at the local coffee house.

**Details for the event:**

We plan to meet at the local coffee house every week to discuss the selected book. The first meeting will take place next Friday, October 20th. We will be reading Alice in Wonderland by Lewis Carroll. Each participant is expected to bring a copy of the book. The session will last approximately two hours. We aim to foster a friendly discussion among all members who attend.

Please RSVP by this Wednesday so we can reserve the correct seating and order refreshments. Looking forward to seeing everyone there!

Best regards,

Alex<|im_end|>
